etymology.description: Depicts a mouth 口 (kǒu, "mouth") at the bottom, with two near-parallel lines 八 (bā, "eight") at the top indicating an open space, such as between mountain walls
etymology.interpretation: The character 㕣 (miǎn, "look over") visually represents a space between two entities, conceptually implying 'to look over' as one gazes across an open area. The relationship with 谷 (gǔ, "valley") emphasizes the notion of being between boundaries, which can relate to observation.
